summary: An excellent SBD/Matrix capture of a high‑energy Cal Expo show – blistering Bertha, legendary Jack Straw and an unforgettable Eyes of the World.
review: The recording is crystal‑clear for a matrix source, offering clean separation with just enough crowd ambience to convey the party atmosphere. Musically the night ranks among the band’s 1990 highlights: a thunderous Bertha opening, killer Jack Straw, soaring Eyes of the World, and Phil’s bomb‑dropping bass speech that silenced the quit‑rumor, all delivered by tight playing from Jerry, Brent and Bill.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 40 reviews overwhelmingly praise both performance (top‑tier show) and sound (excellent matrix clarity); the few minor criticisms do not outweigh the strong consensus.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Unbelievable clarity, clean and crisp mix with just enough audience noise. A few reviewers note occasional left‑channel chatter on Row Jimmy and a slight “swirl” in the mids, but overall the sound is superb.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Starts with a ferocious Bertha and Jack Straw, maintains high energy through an inspired first set, then delivers a tight second set highlighted by Eyes of the World, Box, and Row Jimmy. The pacing feels natural and the crowd’s enthusiasm is palpable.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Tone described as alive, heavy and “berserk” on leads; occasional vocal strain after set 2 noted.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass “dropping bombs”, high in the mix, crisp, and his anti‑rumor speech was a highlight for the crowd.
band_member_comments.Bob: Missed a few vocal cues early (two instances) leading to some dark looks, but overall solid vocals when on point.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland praised for fiery playing – “on fire”, “slapping his keys”, and strong contributions throughout.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming described as tight with a sweet groove, especially during the Gimme Some Lovin' jam.
